---
title: "Labor Market Time Matcher"
format: html
---

```{=html}
<div class="guess-card">

  <h2>📊 Which Period Do Your Labor Market Values Match?</h2>

  <p class="intro">
    Enter the <strong>unemployment rate</strong> and <strong>population value</strong> you are curious about,
    and find out which period in the dataset they are most similar to. 🔍
  </p>

  <div class="input-box">
    <label>Unemployment rate (%)</label>
    <input id="unemployment" type="number" value="8.6" step="0.1">
    <p class="info">ℹ️ Unemployment rate range: <strong>7.0%</strong> – <strong>15.1%</strong></p>
  </div>

  <div class="input-box">
    <label>Population 15 years and over (thousand people)</label>
    <input id="population" type="number" value="58765" step="1">
    <p class="info">ℹ️ Population range: <strong>47,938</strong> – <strong>66,742</strong> thousand people</p>
  </div>

  <button onclick="findClosestYear()">Find Closest Period 🚀</button>

  <h3 id="result"></h3>

</div>

<style>
.guess-card {
  max-width: 720px;
  margin: 40px auto;
  padding: 32px;
  text-align: center;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f7fbff, #eef5ff);
  border: 1px solid #d8e6f8;
  border-radius: 22px;
  box-shadow: 0 10px 28px rgba(40, 80, 130, 0.12);
}

.guess-card h2 {
  color: #1f3b57;
  font-size: 30px;
  margin-bottom: 18px;
}

.intro {
  font-size: 18px;
  line-height: 1.6;
  color: #334155;
  margin-bottom: 28px;
}

.input-box {
  margin: 24px auto;
}

.input-box label {
  display: block;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #243b53;
  margin-bottom: 8px;
}

.input-box input {
  width: 260px;
  padding: 10px 14px;
  text-align: center;
  font-size: 18px;
  border: 2px solid #9bbce3;
  border-radius: 12px;
  outline: none;
}

.input-box input:focus {
  border-color: #2563eb;
  box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(37, 99, 235, 0.15);
}

.info {
  color: #475569;
  font-size: 15px;
  margin-top: 8px;
}

button {
  margin-top: 12px;
  padding: 12px 24px;
  font-size: 17px;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: white;
  background: #2563eb;
  border: none;
  border-radius: 14px;
  cursor: pointer;
}

button:hover {
  background: #1d4ed8;
}

#result {
  margin-top: 28px;
  padding: 24px 28px;
  background: #ffffff;
  border-left: 6px solid #2563eb;
  border-radius: 16px;
  color: #1e293b;
  white-space: pre-line;
  text-align: left;
  font-size: 17px;
  font-weight: 400;
  line-height: 1.55;
}

#result::first-line {
  font-weight: 700;
  font-size: 22px;
}
</style>

<script>
const unemployment = [
10.6,10.4,10.0,9.3,8.4,8.1,8.8,8.2,8.4,9.4,9.1,9.9,
11.2,10.6,10.0,8.6,7.6,7.0,8.8,8.0,8.0,8.6,7.9,8.8,
10.5,9.5,9.4,9.2,7.6,7.4,8.9,7.9,8.6,9.3,9.1,9.7,
10.8,10.7,10.4,8.6,7.9,8.4,8.9,9.0,9.6,10.2,10.7,13.2,
14.1,15.0,14.7,13.4,12.6,11.9,12.0,12.3,13.1,11.7,12.0,13.0,
13.3,14.1,12.6,11.0,10.1,9.8,10.1,10.1,11.2,10.0,9.7,10.6,
11.0,10.9,9.9,9.3,8.5,8.4,8.4,8.2,8.6,7.6,8.7,8.7,
9.3,10.2,9.1,7.9,7.5,7.1,7.1,8.0,8.3,7.8,8.5,9.2,
9.6,10.0,9.2,8.7,8.0,7.7,8.5,9.1,8.8,8.8,9.1,9.2,
10.5,10.5,9.4,8.8,8.4,9.0,9.7,10.1,10.1,10.9,10.4,10.8,
11.9,11.7,10.4,9.6,8.9,9.6,10.0,10.0,10.3,10.5,10.2,10.9,
11.5,11.0,10.1,9.1,9.0,10.1,11.0,11.0,11.8,11.9,11.6,12.8,
13.5,12.9,11.7,10.5,9.7,10.4,10.6,10.6,10.4,10.5,10.0,10.3,
11.0,10.8,9.8,9.3,9.4,10.2,10.7,11.1,11.4,11.7,12.0,13.3,
15.1,15.0,13.9,12.9,12.5,13.4,14.1,14.2,14.0,13.3,12.9,13.6,
14.1,13.5,13.0,12.8,12.6,13.0,14.6,13.0,12.4,12.8,12.9,13.0,
13.4,14.1,13.1,12.9,12.4,10.4,12.1,12.0,11.1,10.7,10.9,11.3,
12.1,11.4,11.4,10.6,10.1,9.7,10.6,9.8,9.9,9.9,9.9,10.4,
10.3,10.6,10.3,10.0,8.8,9.0,9.7,9.2,8.9,8.2,8.8,8.9,
9.8,9.2,8.8,8.5,7.7,8.9,9.3,8.5,8.6,8.5,8.4,8.7,
9.0,8.7,8.0,8.5,7.6,8.5,8.3,8.5,8.6,8.2,8.6,7.8,
8.6,9.0
];

const population = [
47.938,48.015,48.086,48.163,48.239,48.317,48.392,48.470,48.548,48.623,48.701,48.776,
48.854,48.933,49.003,49.081,49.157,49.235,49.311,49.389,49.467,49.543,49.621,49.697,
49.773,49.848,49.916,49.991,50.064,50.139,50.212,50.287,50.362,50.435,50.510,50.583,
50.651,50.712,50.769,50.831,50.890,50.951,51.010,51.071,51.133,51.192,51.253,51.312,
51.393,51.466,51.542,51.626,51.707,51.791,51.872,51.956,52.039,52.120,52.204,52.285,
52.376,52.474,52.561,52.661,52.756,52.854,52.949,53.047,53.145,53.240,53.338,53.433,
53.525,53.610,53.688,53.773,53.856,53.941,54.024,54.109,54.195,54.277,54.363,54.445,
54.528,54.608,54.683,54.763,54.841,54.921,54.998,55.078,55.159,55.236,55.316,55.394,
55.480,55.573,55.658,55.751,55.841,55.935,56.025,56.118,56.212,56.302,56.395,56.486,
56.571,56.648,56.718,56.795,56.869,56.946,57.021,57.098,57.175,57.249,57.326,57.401,
57.475,57.545,57.609,57.679,57.747,57.818,57.891,57.973,58.055,58.134,58.215,58.294,
58.368,58.433,58.493,58.558,58.621,58.686,58.756,58.835,58.914,58.990,59.069,59.146,
59.493,59.567,59.634,59.709,59.781,59.855,59.928,60.002,60.077,60.149,60.223,60.295,
60.360,60.415,60.464,60.519,60.571,60.626,60.679,60.734,60.788,60.841,60.896,60.949,
61.017,61.101,61.177,61.261,61.342,61.426,61.507,61.591,61.675,61.756,61.840,61.921,
62.015,62.119,62.216,62.321,62.421,62.525,62.626,62.730,62.834,62.935,63.039,63.140,
63.236,63.323,63.401,63.488,63.572,63.659,63.744,63.831,63.918,64.002,64.089,64.173,
64.256,64.334,64.405,64.484,64.560,64.639,64.714,64.793,64.871,64.947,65.026,65.102,
65.166,65.214,65.257,65.305,65.352,65.400,65.446,65.494,65.542,65.589,65.637,65.683,
65.726,65.763,65.799,65.834,65.870,65.906,65.942,65.979,66.016,66.051,66.088,66.124,
66.163,66.206,66.245,66.288,66.330,66.373,66.414,66.458,66.501,66.543,66.586,66.627,
66.680,66.742
];

const monthNames = [
  "January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
  "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December"
];

function getMonthYear(index) {
  const startYear = 2005;
  const monthIndex = index % 12;
  const year = startYear + Math.floor(index / 12);

  return monthNames[monthIndex] + " " + year;
}

function getPeriodInfo(index) {
const year = 2005 + Math.floor(index / 12);

  if (year >= 2005 && year <= 2007) {
    return {
      name: "Pre-Global Crisis Growth Period",
      text: "Before the global crisis, economic growth and increasing production supported employment. As businesses expanded and investments increased, unemployment stayed at relatively moderate levels."
    };
  }

  if (year >= 2008 && year <= 2009) {
    return {
      name: "Global Financial Crisis Period",
      text: "The global financial crisis reduced international demand, slowed production, and weakened investments. As companies faced economic uncertainty and lower revenues, unemployment increased significantly in the dataset, especially in 2009."
    };
  }

  if (year >= 2010 && year <= 2013) {
    return {
      name: "Post-Crisis Recovery Period",
      text: "After the crisis, economic activity and production gradually recovered. As firms regained confidence and hiring increased, unemployment declined compared to the crisis years."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2014) {
    return {
      name: "Local Election & Slower Growth Period",
      text: "During this period, economic growth became slower and labor market improvements weakened. Businesses appeared more cautious in hiring decisions, keeping unemployment at moderate levels."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2015) {
    return {
      name: "Parliamentary Elections & Political Uncertainty Period",
      text: "Repeated elections and political uncertainty may have delayed investment and hiring decisions. When firms become uncertain about future economic conditions, they often reduce recruitment and postpone expansion plans."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2016) {
    return {
      name: "Political Shock & Transition Period",
      text: "Political instability and uncertainty negatively affected investor confidence and business expectations. Such conditions can slow economic activity and make firms more cautious about employment decisions."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2017) {
    return {
      name: "Referendum & Currency Pressure Period",
      text: "Rising exchange rate pressure increased production and import costs for many firms. Higher costs and financial uncertainty may reduce companies’ willingness to hire new workers."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2018) {
    return {
      name: "Presidential/Parliamentary Election & Currency Crisis Period",
      text: "The sharp currency crisis increased inflation and raised costs for businesses. As purchasing power weakened and economic uncertainty increased, unemployment rose noticeably, especially toward the end of the year."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2019) {
    return {
      name: "Local Election & Economic Adjustment Period",
      text: "Following the 2018 currency crisis, firms faced weaker demand, high costs, and financial pressure. This adjustment period is associated with some of the highest unemployment levels in the dataset."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2020) {
    return {
      name: "COVID-19 Shock Period",
      text: "Pandemic restrictions, lockdowns, and reduced economic activity disrupted many sectors, especially tourism, retail, and services. Although unemployment did not rise sharply compared to 2019 in the dataset, labor market pressure remained very high."
    };
  }

  if (year >= 2021 && year <= 2022) {
    return {
      name: "Post-COVID Recovery & High Inflation Period",
      text: "As the economy reopened after the pandemic, production and employment began recovering. However, high inflation and rising living costs continued creating pressure on businesses and workers."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2023) {
    return {
      name: "Earthquake, Presidential/Parliamentary Election & Inflation Pressure Period",
      text: "The earthquakes caused major regional disruptions by damaging workplaces, interrupting production, and forcing migration from affected cities. At the same time, election uncertainty and inflation pressure affected business expectations. Despite these challenges, unemployment continued to decline in the overall dataset."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2024) {
    return {
      name: "Local Election & Stabilization Effort Period",
      text: "During this period, economic policies focused more on controlling inflation and stabilizing the economy. Lower unemployment levels in the dataset may reflect a relatively more balanced labor market environment compared to previous crisis periods."
    };
  }

  if (year === 2025) {
    return {
      name: "Disinflation & Labor Market Rebalancing Period",
      text: "As anti-inflation policies continued, the labor market appeared more stable compared to earlier years. Lower unemployment levels suggest a more balanced economic environment in the dataset."
    };
  }

  return {
    name: "Recent Labor Market Period",
    text: "The available 2026 data only covers the beginning of the year, so this period should be interpreted carefully."
  };
}

function findClosestYear() {
  const userUnemployment = Number(document.getElementById("unemployment").value);
  const userPopulationInput = Number(document.getElementById("population").value);
  const userPopulation = userPopulationInput / 1000;
  const resultBox = document.getElementById("result");

  let errorMessages = [];

  if (userUnemployment < 7.0 || userUnemployment > 15.1) {
    errorMessages.push("⚠️ Unemployment rate must be between 7.0% and 15.1%.");
  }

  if (userPopulationInput < 47938 || userPopulationInput > 66742) {
    errorMessages.push("⚠️ Population value must be between 47,938 and 66,742 thousand people.");
  }

  if (errorMessages.length > 0) {
    resultBox.innerText = errorMessages.join("\n");
    resultBox.style.borderLeft = "6px solid #dc2626";
    resultBox.style.background = "#fef2f2";
    return;
  }

  let bestIndex = 0;
  let bestScore = Infinity;

  for (let i = 0; i < unemployment.length; i++) {
    let unemploymentScore = Math.abs(unemployment[i] - userUnemployment) / (15.1 - 7.0);
    let populationScore = Math.abs(population[i] - userPopulation) / (66.742 - 47.938);
    let totalScore = unemploymentScore + populationScore;

    if (totalScore < bestScore) {
      bestScore = totalScore;
      bestIndex = i;
    }
  }

  const periodInfo = getPeriodInfo(bestIndex);

  resultBox.style.borderLeft = "6px solid #2563eb";
  resultBox.style.background = "#ffffff";

resultBox.innerText =
  "✅ Your values are most similar to: " + getMonthYear(bestIndex) +
  "\n\n📉 Unemployment rate: " + unemployment[bestIndex] + "%" +
  "\n👥 Population: approximately " + population[bestIndex].toFixed(1) + " million people" +
  "\n\n📌 Period Context" +
  "\n" + periodInfo.name +
  "\n\n📝 Interpretation" +
  "\n" + periodInfo.text;
}
</script>

<!-- AI Usage: All of the "Guess the Year?" page is created by AI.-->
<!-- AI Prompt: "When we enter population and unemployment rate data, can you create something that shows which year and month the entered values are most similar to?"-->
```
